Tollens' reagent is an alkaline solution of ammoniacal silver nitrate and is used to test for aldehydes. Tollens' test, also known as silver-mirror test, is a qualitative laboratory test used to distinguish between an aldehyde and a ketone. It exploits the fact that aldehydes are readily oxidized (see oxidation), whereas ketones are not.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Vitamin K refers to structurally similar, fat-soluble vitamins found in foods and marketed as dietary supplements. The human body requires vitamin K for post-synthesis modification of certain proteins that are required for blood coagulation or for controlling binding of calcium in bones and other tissues. Due to deficiency of vitamin K causes increases in blood clotting time.

The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory is a model used to predict 3-D molecular geometry based on the number of valence shell electron bond pairs among the atoms in a molecule or ion. This model assumes that electron pairs will arrange themselves to minimize repulsion effects from one another.

solution:
In basic medium, oxidising action of H2O2.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn+4+2OH-

In basic medium, reducing action of H2O2

I2+H2O2+2OH-→2I-+2H2O+O2

In acidic medium, oxidising action of H2O2

PbS(s)+4H2O2(aq)→PbSO4( s)+4H2O(l)

Hence correct option (4).

subject: Mathematics
solution:
LHS of all the options are same i.e. ((P→Q)∧~Q)

≡(~P∨Q)∧~Q

≡(~P∧~Q)∨(Q∧~Q)

≡~P∧~Q

(A) (~P∧~Q)→Q

≡~(~P∧~Q)∨Q

≡(P∨Q)∨Q≠ tautology

(B) (~P∧~Q)→~P

≡~(~P∧~Q)∨~P

≡(P∨Q)∨~P

(C) (~P∧~Q)→P

≡(P∨Q)∨P≠ Tautology

(D) (~P∧~Q)→(P∧Q)

≡(P∨Q)∨(P∧Q)≠ Tautology

subject: Mathematics
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is symmetric, transitive but not reflexive
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is reflexive, symmetric but not transitive
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is reflexive, transitive but not symmetric
solution:
Given A and B are matrices of n×n order and ARB iff there exists a non-singular matrix P detP≠0 such that PAP-1=B.

Reflexivity Check :

ARA⇒PAP-1=A which is true for P=I.

So, R is reflexive relation.

Symmetric Check :

ARB⇒PAP-1=B⇒P-1PAP-1P=P-1BP⇒IAI=P-1BP⇒P-1BP=A⇒BRA for matrix P-1.

So, R is symmetric relation.

Transitivity Check :

ARB⇒PAP-1=B and BRC⇒PBP-1=C.

So, PPAP-1P-1=C.

⇒P2AP2-1=C⇒ARC

So, R is a transitive relation.

Since, R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive all.

Hence, R is an equivalence relation.

⇒P0+mgA+ρgH+12Pv12

=P0+0+12ρv2   ...1

⇒ Neglecting v1

⇒v=8+1.2
